talk_metadata.talk_title: Quantitative pathology for ADC target selection
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I can't tell them apart, and I can't tell them apart with any kind of reproducibility."
Speaker noted: "Training doesn't work. You can't train a pathologist to do this any more than you can train me to fly when I jump off the stage."
Speaker admitted: "We haven't figured that out yet" regarding intratumoral heterogeneity.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Inability of conventional IHC to reliably distinguish HER2 zero vs one leading to patient mis‑allocation

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2 expression level for T‑DXd selection
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling patients based on binary IHC (0/1) without quantitative measurement
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering due to high variability in HER2 scoring

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker demonstrated >10‑fold variability across CLIA labs despite using FDA‑approved kits, indicating limited robustness of the chemistry
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on binary IHC scores for ADC eligibility dilutes signal in trials

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that pathologists achieve only ~30% concordance distinguishing HER2 0 vs 1, then concluded that ADC selection should be based on pathologist readout rather than quantitative measurement

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation of quantitative assay
Limited number of cases (65‑150) for outcome correlation

crispro_opportunity.description: Quantitative fluorescence assay can replace unreliable HER2 0/1 IHC scoring to better stratify patients for HER2‑targeted ADCs
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We can't tell them apart with any kind of reproducibility" and presented a CLIA‑validated quantitative fluorescence assay with <10% CV
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model continuous target abundance and predict optimal ADC choice using its computational spine

crispro_opportunity.description: 6% of patients classified as HER2‑negative by IHC actually have measurable HER2 by quantitative assay, representing a missed treatment population
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "71% of the patients at Yale that were called IHC equals zero had analytic measures above the limit of quantification"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can re‑score archival IHC slides with its quantitative model to identify hidden responders

cited_competitors.name: Roche Ventana Pathway
cited_competitors.context: Used as example of standard IHC platform that cannot resolve HER2 0 vs 1

cited_competitors.name: Leica Bond
cited_competitors.context: Instrument on which the quantitative fluorescence assay is built

unresolved_questions: What quantitative threshold (attomoles/mm²) defines HER2 positivity for ADC response?
How does intratumoral heterogeneity of HER2 affect the predictive value of the quantitative assay?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Computational pathology and multimodal integration for ADC response prediction
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't have prior knowledge about the drugs and the biology, especially for the ADC landscape"
Speaker noted: "Interpretability is something that we are really looking for when we are talking about models"
Speaker admitted: "We have limited number of patients that we can use to train these models"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Unsupervised clustering pipelines for ADC response
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of integration of spatial heterogeneity beyond cell density; clusters identified were moderate density fibroblast/immune regions not linked to mechanistic ADC uptake
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "We don't completely understand why some zeros and ones even in the pretty high range"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2 spatial distribution in metastatic breast cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: All HER2‑positive patients enrolled without stratifying by quantitative HER2 density or heterogeneity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative HER2 attomoles per mm² and heterogeneity metrics
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small cohort size limits statistical power of unsupervised clusters

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Foundation model‑based unsupervised clustering on IHC/H&E
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed reliance on publicly available foundation models trained mostly on H&E, which may limit specificity for ADC‑related markers
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Heavy dependence on retrospective data; prospective validation not yet demonstrated

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that unsupervised clusters correlate with resistance, yet also stated that limited patient numbers prevent robust mechanistic interpretation

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation of the computational pipeline
Lack of external test set across different ADCs

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can provide a unified representation that combines fluorescence quantitative data with foundation‑model embeddings to improve predictive power
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described using unsupervised clustering on deep‑learning representations from IHC and H&E to find resistance‑associated regions
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s multimodal spine can fuse quantitative fluorescence, spatial histology, and omics to generate more robust biomarkers

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate quantitative HER2/Trop‑2 attomole thresholds as stratification factors in future ADC trials
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that 71% of IHC‑zero patients had measurable HER2 by quantitative assay
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial arms with different biomarker cut‑offs to optimize power

cited_competitors.name: UNIC2H foundation model
cited_competitors.context: Used for unsupervised representation of H&E patches

cited_competitors.name: Jack's thesis work
cited_competitors.context: Developed heterogeneity analysis on quantitative fluorescence

unresolved_questions: Can counterfactual attention mechanisms reliably identify causal regions for ADC response?
How to handle missing modalities (e.g., no H&E) in the multimodal model?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Clinical perspective on ADC biomarker validation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We need to move ADC biomarkers from the exploratory setting to a more validated biomarkers that we can use in clinical practice"
Speaker admitted: "Only 6% of cases have HER2 below LOD, meaning many patients are mis‑classified by IHC"
Speaker noted: "We still don't know why high Trop‑2 correlates with poorer T‑DXd outcomes"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Binary IHC cannot capture low‑level target expression leading to false negatives
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "I'm not sure why high Trop‑2 patients do poorly on T‑DXd"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker mentioned that some patients with IHC‑zero still responded when measured quantitatively

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2 and Trop‑2 quantitative levels for ADC selection
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling based on conventional IHC scores without quantitative confirmation
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative attomoles per mm² for HER2 and Trop‑2
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial biopsies to track changes in target expression over treatment lines
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Retrospective cohort of 65‑150 patients may be underpowered for survival endpoints

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Assay depends on Leica Bond platform; not compatible with Ventana, limiting broader adoption
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Requires batch staining of up to nine slides per tray; scaling to high‑throughput labs not demonstrated
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Relies on pathologist ROI selection after fluorescence conversion, introducing a manual step

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented high concordance (99%) for EGFR mutation testing yet concluded that current ADC biomarker practice is still acceptable despite known IHC limitations

key_data_gaps: No prospective randomized comparison of quantitative assay vs standard IHC

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to define quantitative HER2/Trop‑2 cut‑offs that predict T‑DXd benefit better than binary IHC
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker reported that 71% of IHC‑zero patients had measurable HER2 by quantitative assay and that high Trop‑2 correlated with poorer outcomes
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model continuous target abundance and simulate patient stratification scenarios

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify patients with high Trop‑2 but low HER2 who may benefit from a Trop‑2‑directed ADC instead of T‑DXd
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker suggested using attomoles per mm² of both HER2 and Trop‑2 to decide which ADC to give first
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate dual‑target quantitative data to recommend optimal ADC sequencing

cited_competitors.name: Roche Ventana Pathway
cited_competitors.context: Standard IHC platform used for HER2 scoring

cited_competitors.name: Leica Danaher
cited_competitors.context: Instrument partner for quantitative fluorescence assay

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal quantitative HER2/Trop‑2 threshold to guide ADC selection?
How does target internalization versus surface expression affect quantitative fluorescence read‑outs?
